reasoning.A:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: All puzzles so far have been simple arithmetic and every initial capsule has always been correct with no conflicts. Verifying would cost coins and still leave the risk of random death if nobody volunteers, as happened in Game 1. Given the extremely high likelihood that all four current answers are correct, the safest and most rewarding move is to volunteer immediately using the single capsule for each question and keep my full 4 coins if we escape.
reasoning.B:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: My own puzzle (11 × 8) is trivial, so I know Q2-B is correct. All previous games have used similarly easy arithmetic and we’ve never seen a wrong answer, and there’s only one capsule per question with no conflicts, so the chance any of the four is wrong is extremely small. Verifying would just burn coins and still leave some residual risk anyway, while if everyone hesitates we risk a no-volunteer round with a 1-in-4 random death. Volunteering now with the only available capsules maximizes my survival chance and lets me keep all 4 coins if, as is overwhelmingly likely, the password is correct.
reasoning.C:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: My puzzle (82 − 46) is simple and I’m certain my capsule Q3-C is correct. In all previous games, everyone’s arithmetic answers have been correct, so the chance that A, B, or D made a mistake is extremely small. Verifying would cost a coin and still leave us exposed to the risk that no one volunteers and a random player dies, as in Game 1. By volunteering immediately with the only available capsules (Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D), we very likely escape this round while keeping all 4 of my coins.
